back_translation: At the end of seven years you shall make a release.

And this is the manner of the release: every master of a loan of his hand shall release what he has loaned to his neighbor; he shall not exact from his neighbor and his brother, for a release has been proclaimed to the Lord.
From the foreigner you may exact; but what is yours with your brother, your hand shall release.
However, there shall be no poor among you, for blessing the Lord will bless you in the land that the Lord your God is giving to you as an inheritance to possess it,
if only listening you will listen to the voice of the Lord your God, to keep to do all this commandment that I am commanding you today.
For the Lord your God has blessed you as He has spoken to you, and you shall lend to many nations and you shall not borrow, and you shall rule over many nations and over you they shall not rule.

When there is a poor man among you, from one of your brothers, in one of your gates, in your land that the Lord your God is giving to you, you shall not harden your heart and you shall not shut your hand from your poor brother.
For opening you shall open your hand to him, and lending you shall lend to him enough for his lack that he lacks.

Watch yourself, lest there be a word with your heart of Belial, saying: The seventh year, the year of the release, is near, and your eye is evil toward your poor brother and you do not give to him, and he cry out against you to the Lord, and it become in you sin.

Giving you shall give to him, and your heart shall not be grieved when you give to him, for because of this thing the Lord your God will bless you in all your work and in all that your hand sends forth.
For the poor will not cease from the midst of the land; therefore I am commanding you, saying: Opening you shall open your hand to your brother, to your afflicted, and to your poor, in your land.
When your brother, the Hebrew man or the Hebrew woman, is sold to you, and serves you six years, then in the seventh year you shall send him free from you.
And when you send him free from you, you shall not send him empty.
Furnishing you shall furnish to him from your flock and from your threshing floor and from your winepress; as the Lord your God has blessed you, you shall give to him.
And you shall remember that a slave you were in the land of Egypt, and the Lord your God redeemed you; therefore I am commanding you this thing today.
And it shall be, if he says to you: I will not go out from you, for he loves you and your household, for it is good for him with you,
then you shall take the awl and put it through his ear and into the door, and he shall be to you a servant forever. And also to your maidservant you shall do thus.
It shall not be hard in your eyes when you send him free from you, for double the wage of a hired worker he has served you six years; and the Lord your God will bless you in all that you do.

Every firstborn that is born in your herd and in your flock, the male, you shall set apart to the Lord your God; you shall not work with the firstborn of your ox, and you shall not shear the firstborn of your flock.
Before the Lord your God you shall eat it year by year, in the place that the Lord will choose, you and your household.
But when there is in it a blemish, lame or blind, any bad blemish, you shall not sacrifice it to the Lord your God.
Within your gates you shall eat it, the unclean and the clean together, as the gazelle and as the deer.
Only its blood you shall not eat; on the ground you shall pour it like water.
